g.text: REX.B (Bit 0) — ModR/M r/m or SIB Base Extension
When W=1: Operations use 64-bit registers (rax, rbx...) instead of 32-bit (eax, ebx...)
When R=1: ModR/M reg field accesses r8-r15 instead of rax-rdi
When X=1: SIB index field accesses r8-r15 as index registers
When B=1: r/m field accesses r8-r15, or SIB base uses r8-r15 as base
Note: REX prefix only needed for 64-bit operands OR extended registers (r8-r15)
The 3-bit code (0-7) stays the same; REX bit selects upper/lower half of register set
Without REX.W, operations default to 32-bit, zero-extending results to 64 bits!
uint8_t make_rex(bool w, bool r, bool x, bool b) {
